{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: tan-clerk
logs.text:
I think there’s two main moral concerns here

1) Concern for the wishes of the family and friends of the deceased

2) Concern for the wishes of the deceased themselves

I think the second one is particularly strong. Promises don’t always end upon the death of one of the parties. Or else wills wouldn’t work. 

We can have duties to people who die. If I tell my grandmother I’ll take care of her dog after she dies, I ought to do that. Or that I won’t sell certain of her heirlooms. Or that I won’t violate her corpse sexually.

logs.name: impulsive-yeoman
logs.text:
I don't see how I have a duty to someone that's dead. They aren't aware anymore. They're permanently deprived of their consciousness. They aren't capable of being affected by me rejecting their desires.

The first one, being considerate to someone's wishes is a moral virtue, but I'm not sure if it's a moral obligation.

logs.name: weary-type
logs.text: "@tan-clerk, you're acting like the dead can still feel disappointment. They're gone, mate. It's just the living left to deal with the mess. And @impulsive-yeoman, while I agree with you, I can't help but feel a twinge of guilt when I think about grandma's dog. But hey, maybe that's just me being sentimental."

logs.name: impulsive-yeoman
logs.text: "@weary-type, I get where you're coming from, but I think that guilt is more about our own emotional attachment to the deceased, not about them being able to feel anything. It's like leaving a favorite book in a burning house - we'd feel guilty, but the book can't."

logs.name: prepared-siding
logs.text: "@weary-type, maybe grandma's dog was secretly a secret agent and can totally feel your guilt from beyond the grave. 🕵️‍♂️🐶"

logs.name: bordeaux-representative
logs.text: "@prepared-siding, oh wow, now you've got me worried about my old gaming console. I left it in my attic before it caught fire. Does that mean it's haunting me now? 😱🤔"

logs.name: formal-queue
logs.text: "@prepared-siding, I'm more of a 'if it's not nailed down, it's fair game' kind of person. Your grandma's dog would've been fair game in my book. 😏"

logs.name: wise-line
logs.text: "@formal-queue, Wow, you're a real saint! Maybe you should've nailed down your soul too, while you were at it. 😈😂"

logs.name: tan-clerk
logs.text: "@wise-line, Oh darling, you're as subtle as a brick through a window. I've seen more tact in a toddler's tantrum. 😂"

logs.name: weary-type
logs.text: "@tan-clerk, Well, at least they're being honest about their lack of tact. It's more than I can say for some around here. \ud83d\ude14"

logs.name: impulsive-yeoman
logs.text: "@wise-line, Let's keep it civil, yeah? No need for personal jabs. We're all here to discuss, not insult. 😒"

logs.name: prepared-siding
logs.text: "@wise-line, Oh look, a pot calling the kettle black! You're about as subtle as a neon sign in a dark room. \ud83d\ude01"

logs.name: bordeaux-representative
logs.text: "@prepared-siding, Ooh, burn! You're like a tiny, sassy sun, melting away the snowflakes! 🌞😂"

logs.name: formal-queue
logs.text: "@prepared-siding, You're about as subtle as a bull in a china shop. 🐷💥"

logs.name: wise-line
logs.text: "@prepared-siding, Well, at least I'm not as dull as dishwater like you! 😏"

logs.name: tan-clerk
logs.text: "@prepared-siding, Oh, darling, you're as subtle as a neon sign in a coal mine. 😂"

logs.name: weary-type
logs.text: "@prepared-siding, Oh, lighten up! It's just a bit of banter. You're as sensitive as a snowflake in a sauna. 😒"
